rootCauseA: recenterJoins moved a diamond join to its parents' x-midpoint but left the join's DOWNSTREAM chain at its first-pass (subtree-packed, start/center-based) column. So after a merge the trunk (join -> Webhook -> Exit) kept the off-center column while the join shifted, producing an unnecessary horizontal knee on Webhook->Exit (and the trunk looked pulled back toward the board center). Additionally there was no rule forcing a single-out node's child onto the parent's exact x, so any inherited offset propagated as a jog.
fixA: In layout.ts: (1) recenterJoins now computes the delta to the parents' midpoint and drags the join's WHOLE downstream subtree by the same delta via shiftSubtree (stopping at any further join; joins processed shallowest-first so outer re-centering settles before inner). (2) Added alignSingleOutChildren (shallow->deep) that forces every single-out node's non-join child onto the parent's column, guaranteeing a pure straight vertical (fromPoint.x === toPoint.x, zero H run) on every single-child edge including the post-merge trunk and final ->Exit. Join stays centered under its two arms AND its downstream chain now sits straight below it.
fixB: Added computeJoinDrops + JOIN_EXTRA_DROP (48px) in layout.ts: an additive per-depth y-offset pushes every row at/below a join's depth down, so the closure->next-node run is rowHeight-cardHeight+48 (comfortably larger than the normal trunk gap and >= MIN_SEGMENT) while the rest of the trunk keeps its normal gap and rows stay aligned across the canvas.
e2eSummary: All automation e2e green, run SOLO one at a time (ports freed between runs): automation-builder 18/18 (incl. new 'post-merge trunk is STRAIGHT below the join' test); automation-canvas-pan-zoom + list-no-overflow + node-editors + list-detail 21/21; lifecycle + versioning + publish-gate + journey-smoke + broadcast-and-automation 19/19. Web unit suite 190 passed / 4 skipped; automation units 134/134 (layout 30, orthogonal-path 15).
notes: Version bumped 0.41.6 -> 0.41.7. Lint: only the 7 known pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps 'rule not found' errors in web, no new problems. New unit tests added to layout.test.ts (mergeThenTrunk fixture: single-out straight, post-merge trunk follows join x, gap-below-merge > normal). New e2e in automation-builder works purely in path-d space (compares path endpoints, never screen boundingBoxes, to avoid pan/zoom transform). Not committed/pushed per instructions.
